steps.title: Attach each variable to its unit cost
steps.content: $g$ counts 10-ride passes, and each 10-ride pass costs $12.50$, so that part of the cost is $12.50g$. $t$ counts single-ride passes, and each single-ride pass costs $1.50$, so that part of the cost is $1.50t$.

steps.title: Set the total equal to the monthly spending
steps.content: Tony spends $80$ total, so add the two cost expressions and set the sum equal to $80$: $$12.50g+1.50t=80.$$ If you graph the relationship in Desmos, use $x$ for $g$ and $y$ for $t$: $12.50x+1.50y=80$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C reverses the costs, putting $1.50$ on the 10-ride passes and $12.50$ on the single rides. Choice D keeps the costs attached to the correct variables.
